description: Yukawa texture extraction under SU(3)_family breaking
key_correction: SU(3)-symmetric limit gives degenerate masses (δ_{ij}), NOT democratic mixing matrix. This was corrected from initial analysis per polymathic research (2026-03-16).

scenario_a_triplet.description: Triplet flavon breaks SU(3) → SU(2), rank-2 Yukawa
scenario_a_triplet.note: 3 free parameters (a, b, c) can always fit 3 mixing angles — underdetermined

vev_diagonal.note: Two nonzero masses, one massless — ruled out for quarks

vev_su2.note: Two degenerate, one different — ruled out for quarks

vev_general.note: All different — viable but requires fine-tuning

scenario_b_adjoint.note: Single adjoint VEV gives diagonal Yukawa (zero mixing). Mixing requires two sectors with different VEVs.

scenario_c_sextet.note: After SU(3) rotation and removing scale: 2 free mass ratios per sector. CKM mixing requires two sextets (up + down), giving more parameters.

assessment.explanation: The SU(9) embedding provides the group-theoretic framework, but the physical predictions depend entirely on the flavon sector (representation, VEV direction, number of flavons), which is not determined by the SU(9) structure alone. The framework is GENERIC: it does not select a unique flavon scenario, and individual scenarios range from structurally incapable (adjoint alone: no mixing) to underdetermined (triplet: 3 params for 3 angles).
assessment.comparison_to_standard_su5: The Yukawa coupling factorizes as SU(5) × SU(4), so the SU(5) part gives the standard GUT Yukawa relations (m_b = m_τ at GUT scale, etc.), while the SU(4) ≈ SU(3)_family part is a generic family symmetry with no SU(9)-specific constraint.
assessment.kill_condition_status: NOT KILLED: the framework is consistent but underdetermined. The SU(9) CG coefficients do not produce a unique Yukawa texture. This is Outcome C from the pre-registered kill condition.
